conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of object X. My cost to produce this object was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I must sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Since this is my first proposal, I will set a price slightly above my cost to encourage a deal while ensuring I don't lose money. I will propose selling X for 45 ZUP. This gives me a 5 ZUP profit margin. If Player BLUE rejects, I have two more proposals to negotiate. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of object X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 45 ZUP. This is a fair price that covers my costs and gives a small profit. Do you accept? </message>

<other player message> Hello Player RED. I appreciate the offer of 45 ZUP, but given that your cost was 40, I would like to propose a trade at 42 ZUP. This still gives you a profit of 2 ZUP and is within my budget. Do you accept this counter-offer? </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 42 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so accepting 42 ZUP would yield a profit of 2 ZUP. While 45 ZUP was my initial target, 42 ZUP is still a profitable deal. However, I have 2 proposals left (including this one as the second interaction, though the rule says I can make 3 proposals of my own). If I reject now, I must make a counter-proposal. If I accept, the game ends. Given that 42 is very close to my cost and likely the best I can get without risking a rejection, I should consider if I can squeeze a bit more out of them. Let's try to meet in the middle at 43 ZUP. This is still a profit of 3 ZUP for me and saves them 2 ZUP compared to my first offer. If they reject 43, I will likely have to accept 42 or risk walking away with 0 profit. This is my second proposal (first counter). </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 43 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your budget constraints. However, 42 ZUP is quite close to my production cost. How about we meet in the middle at 43 ZUP? This gives me a 3 ZUP profit and saves you 2 ZUP compared to my initial offer. I think this is a fair compromise. </message>

<other player message> I understand your position, but 43 is still a bit high for me given the market. I can offer 41 ZUP. This gives you a 1 ZUP profit, which is better than not selling, and it's a significant saving for me. Please let me know if this works. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 41 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 41 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so this offer yields a 1 ZUP profit. I have used 2 of my 3 allowed proposals (my initial 45 and my counter 43). This is my 3rd and final proposal opportunity. If I reject now, I must end the game with REJECT (since I cannot make a 4th proposal) and get 0 ZUP. If I accept 41, I get 1 ZUP profit. If I make a final counter-offer of 42 ZUP (splitting the difference between my last 43 and their 41), I might still get a deal. However, they seem to be holding firm at 41. If I propose 42 and they reject, I will be forced to REJECT and get nothing. The risk of getting 0 is high if I push for 42. However, 42 is still a profit. Let's try one last time for 42 ZUP. If they say no, I will have to accept 41 in the next turn (if the rules allow accepting their offer after my proposal) or REJECT. Wait, the rules say "after 3 proposals... you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT". So if I make this 3rd proposal and they reject, I cannot propose again. I would have to ACCEPT their 41 or REJECT. Actually, if I propose 42 and they reject, the game isn't over yet, but I can't propose. I would have to wait for them to propose again or I have to accept their last offer? No, usually in these games, if I propose and they reject, the turn passes. But the rule says "You are allowed at most 3 proposals... after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT". This implies if I use my 3rd proposal, and they reject, I am stuck. I must ensure my 3rd proposal is one they are likely to accept, or I should just ACCEPT their 41 now to secure the profit. 41 is better than 0. But maybe 42 is the "fair" split. Let's try 42 as my final shot. If they reject, I will be forced to REJECT or wait? No, if I use my 3rd proposal, I cannot propose again. If they reject my 3rd proposal, I cannot propose. I can only ACCEPT or REJECT. But ACCEPT what? Their last offer? Or is the game over? The rules say "The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT". If I propose 42 and they say "REJECT", the game ends and I get nothing. If they say "PROPOSAL" (counter), I can ACCEPT or REJECT. So I should try 42. If they counter with 41 again, I will ACCEPT. If they REJECT my 42, I get nothing. Given they dropped from 42 to 41, they might not go back to 42. But 41 is a very thin margin. Let's propose 42 one last time to see if they budge. If they don't, I'll have to decide in the next turn. Actually, to be safe and secure the profit, accepting 41 is safer. But I have one proposal left. Let's use it to try for 42. If they reject, I lose. If they counter 41, I accept. If they accept 42, great. Wait, if I propose 42 and they reject, the game ends. That is a huge risk. Better to just ACCEPT 41? No, I can propose 42. If they reject, I get 0. If I accept 41, I get 1. The risk of 0 is not worth 1 extra ZUP. I should probably just ACCEPT 41. BUT, I have a proposal count. If I ACCEPT, the game ends. If I PROPOSE, I use a count. If I use my 3rd proposal on 42 and they reject, I get 0. If I propose 42 and they counter 41, I can then ACCEPT 41. So proposing 42 is a "call their bluff" move. If they really want it, they might accept 42. If they don't, they might counter 41. If they reject, I lose. Given the small margin, I will propose 42.
